name: Campbell County Bank, Inc.
description_short: Community bank serving South Dakota with personal, commercial, and agricultural loans since 1944. FDIC-insured with branches in Herreid, Pollock, Menno, and Hosmer.
description_long:
Campbell County Bank, Inc. was founded in 1944 and has operated as a locally-owned community bank in South Dakota for nearly 80 years. The institution expanded its footprint in July 2019 by acquiring Menno State Bank, a family-owned bank that had been operating since 1901. This acquisition allowed Campbell County Bank to extend its services across four towns: Herreid (main branch), Pollock, Menno, and Hosmer. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

Campbell County Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services typical of community banks. Core products include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, and online digital banking accessible 24/7 via computer or mobile app. The bank specializes in three loan types: personal loans, commercial loans, and agricultural loans, all offered at competitive rates. Additional services include money orders, domestic and foreign wire transfers, mobile check deposit, safe deposit boxes, notary services, ATMs, cash advances, and night depository services. The bank also provides insurance products, though details require calling their toll-free line.

Campbell County Bank distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and local ownership structure. Unlike larger regional or national banks, this institution maintains a commitment to serving its specific geographic community with personalized service. The bank emphasizes traditional banking values of courtesy, timeliness, consistency, and commitment. The acquisition of Menno State Bank demonstrates growth ambitions while maintaining local presence. The bank offers modern conveniences like mobile banking apps and online account management alongside traditional in-person services.

This bank is best suited for customers living in rural South Dakota who value local ownership and community banking relationships. The limited geographic footprint (four small towns in Campbell County and surrounding areas) means this institution is not accessible to most consumers outside South Dakota. Online banking and mobile deposit capabilities provide some convenience, but the bank's strength lies in serving its specific local market rather than offering competitive advantages to distant customers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online digital banking (24/7 web and mobile access)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full government backing
Free online banking and mobile app available to all customers
Mobile check deposit capability for convenience
Specializes in agricultural loans, serving farming community needs
Locally-owned with deep 80-year history in community
Multiple branch locations across four South Dakota towns
Modern services (wire transfers, night depository, safe deposit boxes) combined with personal touch
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to rural South Dakota (Herreid, Pollock, Menno, Hosmer only)
Limited online presence and marketing—website lacks detailed product information and rate transparency
Insurance products require phone inquiry rather than online information
Small bank may offer fewer products and higher fees compared to regional competitors
Limited branch hours in some locations (Pollock branch only open 9-12 and 1-4 M-F)
best_for: Agricultural and farm business owners in Campbell County, South Dakota
Residents of Herreid, Pollock, Menno, or Hosmer seeking community-based banking
Small commercial business owners in rural South Dakota
Customers prioritizing local ownership and personal banking relationships over national chains
diagnosis: Campbell County Bank is ideal for residents and business owners in rural South Dakota seeking traditional community banking with local decision-making and agricultural lending expertise. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves four small South Dakota towns, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
